info.title: Voucherify API - Segments
info.description: Voucherify promotion engine REST API. Please see https://docs.voucherify.io/docs for more details.

get.description: Retrieves the segment with a given segment ID.

404.description: Returns an error when requesting a segment that has been deleted or does not exist.

value.details: Cannot find segment with id seg_Mv5w1b2FIEbiCCO5ioT8TfI

delete.description: This method deletes a customer segment.

204.description: Returns no content if deletion is successful.

404.description: Returns an error indicating that the customer segment with given ID was not found.

value.details: Cannot find segment with id seg_DNAOhUtJffvX0f57ajLMFBY

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Apply this filter to order the segments according the date and time when they were created. By default, lists the segments from the newest to the oldest. Use `created_at` to list from the oldest to the newest.

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: Cursor used for paging. Returns results based on `created_at`. Use `order=created_at` query parameter to return results after the date. Use `order=-created_at` query parameter to return results before the date.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Contains the list of segments in the sequence defined by the `order` parameter.

200.description: Returns a `data` array with segment objects.

post.description:
Create a customer segment.

> 🚧 Limit on static segments
>
> There is a cap on the number of customers that you can assign to a static segment: **20,000**. If you would like to create a bigger segment, then you can use the unlimited `auto-update` or `passive` segment instead and use some customer metadata to build this segment.

> 🚧 Limit on Active and Passive segments
>
> You can create a maximum of 100 passive and active segments.

requestBody.description: Specify the boundary conditions for the customer segment.

value.name: Customers in a new Dynamic Segment
Customers in a new Dynamic Segment
Customers in a new Dynamic Segment

key.description: Short string describing the kind of error which occurred.

message.description: A human-readable message providing a short description of the error.

details.description: A human-readable message providing more details about the error.

request_id.description: This ID is useful when troubleshooting and/or finding the root cause of an error response by our support team.

resource_id.description: Unique resource ID that can be used in another endpoint to get more details.

error.description: Includes additional information about the error.

message.description: The message configured by the user in a validation rule.

conditions.description: Data filters used to narrow down the data records to be returned in the result.

$in.description: Array of resource values that should be included in the results (multiple values).

$not_in.description: Array of resource values that should be included in the results (multiple values).

$is.description: Value is exactly this value (single value).

$has_value.description: Value is NOT null. The value for this parameter is an empty string.

$is_unknown.description: Value is null. The value for this parameter is an empty string.

$starts_with.description: Value starts with the specified string.

$ends_with.description: Value ends with the specified string.

created_at.description: Timestamp representing the date and time when the segment was created. The value is shown in the ISO 8601 format.

object.description: The type of the object represented by JSON. This object stores information about segments.

data_ref.description: Identifies the name of the attribute that contains the array of segments.

total.description: Total number of segments returned for given filters and query parameters with the exception of `limit`.

Segment.title: Segment

created_at.description: Timestamp representing the date and time when the segment was created. The value is shown in the ISO 8601 format.

updated_at.description: Timestamp in ISO 8601 format indicating when the segment was updated.

type.description:
Defines whether the segment is:
- Active (`auto-update`): customers enter and leave the segment based on the defined filters and the `customer.segment.entered` and `customer.segment.left` events are triggered,
- Passive (`passive`): customers enter and leave the segment based on the defined filters, but the `customer.segment.entered` and `customer.segment.left` events are not triggered,
- Static (`static`): manually selected customers.

filter.description: Defines a set of criteria for an `auto-update` or `passive` segment type.

object.description: The type of the object represented by JSON. This object stores information about the customer segment.

Segment.description: This is an object representing a customer segment.

Junction.title: Junction
Junction.description: Logical Operator Between Filters. Filter by conditions set on the `junction` parameter indicating how the `conditions` should be accounted for in the query. An `AND` is an all-inclusive logical operator, meaning the `AND` operator displays a record if **ALL** the conditions separated by AND are TRUE, while  an `OR` operator displays a record if **ANY** of the conditions separated by OR is TRUE.

FilterConditionsString.title: Filter by conditions
FilterConditionsString.description: Data filters used to narrow down the data records to be returned in the result.

$in.description: Array of resource values that should be included in the results (multiple values).

$not_in.description: Array of resource values that should be included in the results (multiple values).

$is.description: Value is exactly this value (single value).

$has_value.description: Value is NOT null. The value for this parameter is an empty string.

$is_unknown.description: Value is null. The value for this parameter is an empty string.

$starts_with.description: Value starts with the specified string.

$ends_with.description: Value ends with the specified string.

$not_contain.description: Value does NOT include the specified string.

FilterConditionsDateTime.title: Filter by conditions
FilterConditionsDateTime.description: Data filters used to narrow down the data records to be returned in the result.

$after.description: Value is after this date. The value for this parameter is shown in the ISO 8601 format.

$before.description: Value is before this date. The value for this parameter is shown in the ISO 8601 format.

$has_value.description: Value is NOT null. The value for this parameter is an empty string.

$is_unknown.description: Value is null. The value for this parameter is an empty string.

$more_than.description: Value is more days ago before the current date and time, e.g. more than `10` days ago.

$less_than.description: Value is less days before the current date and time, e.g. less than `10` days ago.

SegmentsCreateRequestBodyStatic.title: Static Customer Segment
SegmentsCreateRequestBodyStatic.description: Request body schema for creating a static customer segment in **POST** `v1/segments`.

type.description: Defines that the segment is static, meaning it includes only the manually selected customers.

SegmentsCreateRequestBodyDynamic.title: Active or Passive Customer Segment
SegmentsCreateRequestBodyDynamic.description: Request body schema for creating an active or passive customer segment in **POST** `v1/segments`.

type.description:
Defines whether the segment is:
- Active (`auto-update`): customers enter and leave the segment based on the defined filters and the `customer.segment.entered` and `customer.segment.left` events are triggered,
- Passive (`passive`): customers enter and leave the segment based on the defined filters, but the `customer.segment.entered` and `customer.segment.left` events are not triggered.

filter.description: Defines a set of criteria for an `auto-update` or `passive` segment type.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Limits the number of objects to be returned. The limit can range between 1 and 100 items. If no limit is set, it returns 10 items.
